content.problemStatement:
A binary string SSS is said to be good, if there exist some kkk binary strings P1,P2,…,PkP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_kP1​,P2​,…,Pk​ such that:


S=P1+P2+…+PkS = P_1 + P_2 + \ldots + P_kS=P1​+P2​+…+Pk​.

Here, +++ denotes string concatenation.
Each PiP_iPi​ has even length.
Each PiP_iPi​ contains only a single distinct character.
For example, 1100,11,1100, 11, 1100,11, and 110000111100001111000011 are all good, while 1,1001,1, 1001, 1,1001, and 101010101010101010 are not.

You are given a binary string SSS of length NNN. NNN is **even**.

You can perform the following operation on it:


Select an alternating subsequence of SSS, and flip all its characters.

That is, turn 000's to 111's and vice versa.

Note that it is allowed to choose the empty subsequence, which has length 000 (and hence leave SSS unchanged).
Is it possible to turn SSS into a good string by performing this operation **exactly once**?

If it is, also find a way to perform this operation.

**Note**: A binary string is called alternating if no two of its adjacent characters are equal. For example, the string 010010010 is alternating, while the string 010001000100 is not.

Specifically, a string of length 111 is alternating.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — denoting length of the binary string.
The next line contains a binary string SSS of length NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, output your answer in the following format.



If it is not possible to make the string good after performing exactly one operation, print −1−1−1.
Otherwise, output two lines.

The first line should contain an integer MMM (0≤M≤N0 \leq M \leq N0≤M≤N) — where MMM is the length of the subsequence selected.
The second line should contain MMM **distinct** space-separated integers between 111 and NNN — the indices of the selected subsequence.

If M=0M = 0M=0, both printing an empty line or not printing it will be accepted.



If there are multiple possible solutions, you may find any of them.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053\cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Choose just the first character and flip it, to obtain S=11S = 11S=11 which is good.
Test case 222: Given S=0110S = 0110S=0110.

Select indices 222 and 444 for the operation. The resulting string is 001100110011 which is good.

Test case 333: SSS is already good.
